page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the 64-bit ARM architecture (arm64), this server features an AWS Neoverse-V1 CPU with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks x86-specific vector extensions like AVX or AMX. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the c7gn.large demonstrates moderate single-core performance but shows limitations in multi-core scaling and memory throughput. The stress-ng single-core CPU test yielded 1532.89, placing it in the average tier, while the multi-core score of 3043.47 falls into the weak category. Similarly, Geekbench single-core performance reached 1514.0, but the multi-core score was limited to 2757.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only large block speed of 57.94 GB/sec, though write speeds and small-block cached reads are relatively weak. Cryptographic performance is also constrained, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 274.06.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7gn.large offers a specialized hardware profile that trades raw multi-threaded compute power and memory capacity for dedicated ARM cores and optimized networking capabilities, featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. This makes the instance highly suitable for network-bound workloads, single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, and basic text processing tasks that benefit from dedicated ARM resources without requiring massive memory footprints or local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an ARM-based AWS Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this arm64 server is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, 32 MB of L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1532.89 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1514.0, though multi-core scaling and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. This resource profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for network-optimized, single-threaded, or lightweight ARM-native workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, text processing, and network-bound utility tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.large is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 2 dedicated Graviton vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight network-bound workloads.

tagline: An ARM-based, network-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated compute cores for lightweight single-threaded and network-bound workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Average single-core performance suitable for single-threaded tasks
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and network-bound workloads
